rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I want to make it clear, although this may have started as an NCI idea, it is not a purely NCI project."
Speaker said: "I am concerned that I don't think a lot of America understands what the National Cancer Institute does..."
Speaker said: "I would say the damage was on the basis of misinformation and incorrect information about how some of these decisions were made..."
Speaker said: "I think we will continue to be in a situation where nine out of 10 grants are not going to get funded, but that's not new."

key_data_gaps: No quantitative outcome data (OS, PFS, response rates) presented

crispro_opportunity.description: Dr. Lattai highlighted the need to develop and validate functional precision medicine assays using patient tumor tissue, noting logistical challenges such as tissue preservation and lack of standardized workflows.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We need to do a better job of demonstrating that functional precision medicine works so that then the tissues that we need will become more easily available."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi-omics and phenotypic screening data to create predictive models that prioritize functional assay readouts, accelerating validation and standardization.

crispro_opportunity.description: The NCI vaccine program will fund three clinical trials to generate go/no‑go signals for therapeutic cancer vaccines, indicating an area where existing assets have limited data and could benefit from computational prioritization.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We are going to fund three different clinical trials over the next three years that will give us some go, no‑go signals in the cancer vaccine field."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze early trial biomarker data to identify patient sub‑populations most likely to respond, informing go/no‑go decisions and potential combination strategies.

crispro_opportunity.description: Discussion of slow US trial activation compared to Australia suggests an opportunity to redesign trial contracting and IRB processes; CrisPRO could model optimal enrollment criteria to reduce dilution of signal.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Australia uses central contracting language and shared IRBs, which cuts activation time dramatically."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial enrollment scenarios, recommending biomarker‑driven eligibility criteria that improve signal detection while maintaining feasibility.

unresolved_questions: How can the NCI quantitatively demonstrate the predictive value of functional precision medicine assays before large‑scale rollout?
What specific biomarkers will be used to stratify patients in the upcoming cancer vaccine trials?

assets_to_track: Therapeutic cancer vaccine trials funded by NCI
